additional_learning: Tablescaping (and/or various techniques within it such as choosing or putting together centerpieces, folding napkins, learning different linen effects (how do they combine the tablecloth with chiffon and hold it in place to create that flowing look?) using food as decor, etc.) Bouquet Making (learning not just arrangement, but how to hold it together),
general_suggestions: Things were a little tight. I had to double check what supplies was mine vs my neighbor's. Maybe spreading out, just a little.
testimonial: Fall Flower Arranging was fantastic. I learned two very different arrangements as well as gained a plethora of knowledge that I could take with me to create my own arrangements in the future!

general_suggestions: Absolutely nothing to improve on! Mike Lee is very good at keeping the attention of the class & answering all our questions.
testimonial: We took the Wine Classes last year with Mike Lee and enjoyed them very much! Knew we would be attending again this year. We met two people who drive over from Decatur after work and have struck up a friendship with them. Mike does an amazing job explaining the different wines. How to look at them and taste them. Always speaks in a way everyone can understand. What a gem!

additional_learning: Maybe another wine course, we really enjoyed this one.
general_suggestions: I wish it was just like 15-30 mins later. It's a little hard to get there right at six when work ends at 5. Also make the note about bringing your own snacks a bit larger, some people didn't know.
testimonial: Mike was a wonderful instructor who was so great at teaching us about the idiosyncrasies of wines from France but never got stuffy about it. Affable and entertaining, we learned so much from such a short class, and we met several great people in the process. Highly recommend if you have any interest in wine.
We had a great time! Looking forward to receiving the recipes from Leah

Paul's class got me in touch with creativity that has been dormant but wanting for a loooong time. He had enticing resources that called forth my muse, and hands-on exercises that kept me engaged and excited. The Allerton landscape was the perfect atmosphere to inspire and integrate the new information during and after each class. I love that the Folk School is harnessing and celebrating the unique talents of our vibrant local community.
The class was great! I learned a lot of new skills and was able to leave with two completed projects.
I've tried to learn guitar many times and this is the first time it started to "click" and I can see myself able to play if I keep practicing
marketing_channels: Allerton's website, Allerton's enewsletter, Allerton's Facebook page

additional_learning: I like your offerings, nice variety!
general_suggestions: This class could have been a little longer.
testimonial: The class I took at Allerton was wonderful! I learned a lot and the instructor was fantastic. It's also great to be able to meander around the gardens before or after the class. A fabulous experience in every way!!!

general_suggestions: Better credit card reader for the bar
testimonial: A delightful date night. Thank you to the organizers!

It would have been good for each table to have a picture of the faces that we were to complete. It was hard to remember the demonstration and then have to do it on our own.
testimonial: The instructor did give some good baking/icing tips.

general_suggestions: A problem I had in the stone painting - the lighting was not good after the sun went down wished I had brought a " desk" light run by batteries as I did not see any close plug. If it was during the day that wouldn't be a problem
testimonial: My daughter in law & I enjoyed the class. She used only the materials Sara provided and created a cute little bird. I had a project in mind so I brought my paint colors and brushes I would need. We would do more of her Santorini stones

satisfaction_comment: The instructor gave us 15 grams of metal clay which is enough for the intended project. And, metal clay is expensive. She had us make a slurry with about 1/2 gram of clay and had us paint the clay onto small leaves. This process is long and tedious as you should paint many layers and dry them in between. During that process, she showed us how to make molds which took 20 minutes to dry in the same oven that we were using. So, we all had to wait. When it came to the torch firing, she had a brand new torch that she didn't know how to fill. At the end of the day, I still have 14 1/2 grams of metal clay and no pendant or earrings. This class was poorly planned and the objectives were not met.
general_suggestions: If the instructor would like to teach a silver metal clay class again, I have experience and would be happy to be an assistant.
testimonial: Unfortunately, I was very disappointed with the outcome. I spent over $170, expecting to get a pendant and earrings. I was looking forward to learning from a new instructor and making some new jewelry. At this point, I need to work up my 14 1/2 grams of metal clay before it dries out. For me, I have the tools and experience. For the rest of the class, they are left with metal clay that they don't know how to use.

testimonial: Cordage was a really interesting and fun hands on workshop. The fall weather was perfect for foraging for materials and the historical context was so interesting.

satisfaction_comment: Love, love, love the piece I created. Will definitely take another barn quilt class when a different design is offered. Jane did a good job teaching the class and responding to questions.
additional_learning: I am fairly new to The Allerton website. I am almost positive there are other offerings that I am interested in; just wish I lived closer. My impression is that classes cover a wide variety of subjects and seem to be reasonably priced. Will check the class schedule next spring for anything to do with gardening or fabric quilting.
general_suggestions: Because I live 140 miles away and the class started at 8:00 a.m. I opted to stay overnight the night before the class. The "Gate House" needs some TLC. Nothing huge, just little things that need repair, painting, replacing. I realize money is tight...perhaps volunteers or students could take on some minor tasks.
testimonial: This was my first time at The Allerton. What a beautiful property! I am a tree hugger and love being out experiencing nature. The next time I visit I will schedule more time to walk and explore. A big thank you to a friend who introduced me to this lovely experience.

satisfaction_comment: Ryan Pankau is the most engaging, informative and entertaining teacher I've experienced in a long, long time!
testimonial: Ryan Pankau is the most engaging, informative and entertaining teacher I've experienced in a long, long time!

additional_learning: more of the melt system art courses - watercolor, drawing
testimonial: I attended this MELT session hoping to find some relief from hip pain and it did NOT disappoint! I came away from that class pain free for the first time in years and can't wait to continue using what I learned! Instructor was awesome!
marketing_channels: Allerton's Facebook page, U of I's eweek

general_suggestions: Yes, whoever manages the heating system should make sure the room is at a comfortable temperature. Maybe check it a hour before class begins. That's not necessarily the instructors responsibility.
testimonial: The instructor was very patient and so informative about hand work. She was enthusiastic and loves what she does. That really carries over to instruction if material. She also used different learning techniques which, I think, everyone benefited from. I love that Allerton is doing this!Thank you
Mary is a wonderful and encouraging instructor who makes the class not only healing, but fun. She meets each student at their individual fitness level and emphasizes self-care and kindness.
Kari is a very good teacher. This class is great value for the money.

additional_learning: The ornothology class looks fun. Id like to maybe take a for raging class
testimonial: My wife and I took the inrto to tarot reading class as a date night. We met some nice people and learned so much. We can't wait to take another class.

satisfaction_comment: I understand the speaker has two other courses, I would be interested in both.
additional_learning: I am more interested in attending programs about nature and the environment than I am about art and music, but am pleased that you offer a wide variety of courses of varied interests.
testimonial: Allerton is the perfect venue for learning about art and nature as it blends them both so perfectly, inside the mansion and throughout the landscape.
marketing_channels: Allerton's website, Allerton's enewsletter, Allerton's Facebook page

testimonial: The instructor was professional, extremely knowledgeable, and fun! He had obviously spent time making sure his presentation would appeal to a wide audience. In addition, he provided us with a great bibliography, links and suggestions to other sites on this subject, suggestions for further explorations, and volunteered to personally give us a walk to observe owls next time we were in his area. Loved the videos of the owls at all ages and stages. As in all classes I have taken at the Farms, this was an excellent opportunity to experience personal growth in mind, soul, and body. We're so lucky to have the amazing resource that is Allerton Park. Thanks to those many staff members who make it the great place it is for all the rest of us.
marketing_channels: Allerton's website, Allerton's enewsletter, From a friend, Smile Politely

additional_learning: I thought it be a great follow up to search for owls in Allerton.
testimonial: I found the stories about Charles and Sarah to be so heartwarming that when I found out Sarah passed Imy eyes started watering. I did not know anything about owls and the approach to learning about them was great.

general_suggestions: maybe a sign outside the classroom letting people know it was a classroom and to not disturb or try to walk in during instruction would probably be a good thing.
testimonial: It felt like everything was in Greek until the third week, and then everything just clicked and I completely understood everything Sharon was talking about. The terminology, reading the patterns, how to setup the loom, everything came together in my head

additional_learning: Growing herbs along with information on their benefits ways they can be used.
testimonial: I appreciate the opportunity to share experiences with family members.
I am always amazed by the depth of knowledge, patience, and kindness of ALL the Allerton teachers and staff. I feel fortunate to have learned so much in such a beautiful setting!
marketing_channels: Allerton's website, Allerton's enewsletter, From a friend, U of I's eweek, Smile Politely

satisfaction_comment: I was pleased with the instruction, a mix of classroom time and outdoor time. Also enjoyed the sorts of folks who attended.
additional_learning: Anything centering the BIPOC experience with folk arts, natural skills, etc would be very cool to experience. I recognize that it might be challenging to pull off, but I am a young black woman and would like to see a more diverse set of audiences experience the awesomeness at Allerton!
testimonial: I really love the opportunity to learn new and different things at the farm. There's not a lot of places where adults can continue to learn interesting and creative subjects. The Farms is such a beautiful backdrop to the sort of creative learning that makes us better, more well-rounded humans.

general_suggestions: No, your staff dies a great job.
testimonial: I wasn't sure quite if signing up to attend the wreath making class by myself was going to fun. No worries there. I immediately felt welcome in the class room and even made some new friends. The teacher was very attentive and I learned a lot.
The instructor was real, easily related to his students. Mr. Lee was able to teach his knowledge with humor and interesting experience.
Art, music, gardening, sewing

additional_learning: Would love to attend another Wine Pairing Dinner, especially with the Luminary Chefs
testimonial: Where else can you learn about such a variety of subjects from experts, all in such a beautiful venue?
